cards.name: The Platinum Card

verdict.text: Keep if you use the credits + fly

earn_rates.category: Flights — direct or via Amex Travel (first $500k/yr)

cards.earn_note: The 1× base is the real weakness — put everyday, dining and grocery spend on a 2× card (Venture X) and use the Platinum for flights and prepaid hotels.

points.note: Cashing out at 0.6¢ is a trap — always transfer. MR is the only bank currency that moves to Delta SkyMiles 1:1.

transfer_partners.highlight: Deepest airline list of any bank currency. Sweet spots: ANA, Virgin Atlantic, Flying Blue, Avios, Aeroplan. Weak spot: only 3 hotel partners.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: Recent cuts: Emirates devalued to 5:4 (Sep 2025), Etihad removed (Jun 30, 2026), Hawaiian removed.

credits.name: Uber Cash
credits.subtitle: $15/mo + $20 Dec
credits.description: Monthly Uber Cash for U.S. rides and Uber Eats.
tips: Add the Platinum in the Uber app wallet — the cash won't appear until you do.
December is $35, not $15 — time a bigger order then.

credits.name: Digital Entertainment
credits.subtitle: $25/mo
credits.description: Monthly credit across Disney+, Hulu, ESPN+, Peacock, Paramount+, NYT, WSJ, YouTube Premium & TV.
tips: Bill annual plans monthly instead — a lump-sum annual charge blows past the $25 cap.
Stack two services to hit exactly $25/mo.

credits.name: CLEAR Plus
credits.subtitle: airport security
credits.description: Covers a CLEAR+ membership at 55+ airports.
tips: Pair with your Global Entry credit for the fastest lane combo.
$0 to you if you don't fly through CLEAR airports — drag the slider down.

credits.name: Fine Hotels + Resorts
credits.subtitle: $300 × 2 · semi-annual
credits.description: Semi-annual credit on prepaid FHR / Hotel Collection stays via Amex Travel. FHR spans ~1,700+ luxury properties with guaranteed perks: breakfast for two, room upgrade, noon check-in, 4pm checkout, and a $100 property credit.
tips: FHR works on ONE-night stays — book a single night to trigger both the $300 credit AND the $100 property credit.
Semi-annual is the catch: you must spend in both H1 and H2.
The ~1,700-property network is the widest of any card program.

credits.name: Resy Dining
credits.subtitle: $100/quarter · enroll
credits.description: Quarterly credit at 10,000+ U.S. Resy restaurants (Amex owns Resy). No reservation needed — just pay with the card.
tips: 10,000+ restaurants in 1,600+ cities is broad, unlike Chase's ~400-restaurant list.
Quarterly with no rollover — set four reminders so a missed quarter doesn't cost you $100.
Resy skews trendy/urban; scout your area in the app to confirm your usual spots qualify.

credits.name: Airline Fee Credit
credits.subtitle: one airline · incidentals
credits.description: Incidental fees (checked bags, seat assignments, day passes) on one airline you select each year.
tips: The old United TravelBank cash-loading trick DIED in mid-2026 — you now need real incidentals.
Select your airline in-account first; covers bags/seats, not airfare.
If you fly carry-on-only basic economy, this is now hard to fully use — set the slider low.

credits.name: Lululemon
credits.subtitle: $75/quarter
credits.description: Quarterly credit at U.S. Lululemon (outlets excluded).
tips: Stack with the Rakuten portal for cash back on the same purchase.
If you'd never shop there, leave the slider at $0.

credits.name: Oura Ring
credits.subtitle: ring hardware only
credits.description: Credit toward an Oura Ring at ouraring.com.
tips: Covers the ring, not the subscription — effectively one-time. $0 unless you're buying a ring.

credits.name: Equinox
credits.subtitle: gym or Equinox+ app
credits.description: Credit toward Equinox or the Equinox+ app.
tips: The Equinox+ app (~$40/mo) can absorb it without a full gym membership. Worthless if you don't use Equinox.

credits.name: Uber One
credits.subtitle: membership
credits.description: Covers an auto-renewing Uber One membership.
tips: Only worth it if you'd pay for Uber One anyway (free delivery + ride discounts).

credits.name: Walmart+
credits.subtitle: monthly membership
credits.description: Covers a Walmart+ membership (bundles Paramount+).
tips: The bundled Paramount+ doubles as an entertainment service if you'd use it.

credits.name: Saks — REMOVED
credits.subtitle: gone Jul 1, 2026
credits.description: The $100/yr Saks credit was permanently removed for all cardholders on July 1, 2026.
tips: If any benefits list still shows Saks, it's out of date — it's gone.

cards.protection_note: Amex does cover emergency medical evacuation/transport at no cost (uncapped) when coordinated through Premium Global Assist. It carries a Baggage Insurance Plan for lost/damaged/stolen bags (secondary) — only baggage delay is missing. Purchase protection here is the strongest of the four.
cards.rental_note: The Amex Platinum's complimentary coverage is secondary in the U.S. (primary only when you rent abroad). Its Premium Car Rental Protection gives true primary coverage but it's a separate paid product (~$19.95–$24.95 per rental). Easiest move: charge rentals to the CSR or Venture X.

status_perks.name: Hilton Honors Gold
status_perks.note: The useful one — free breakfast/food credit and space-available upgrades at most Hiltons.

status_perks.name: Marriott Bonvoy Gold
status_perks.note: Mid-tier: 25% bonus points and 2pm late checkout, but no free breakfast or suite upgrades.

status_perks.name: Global Lounge Collection
status_perks.note: Best in class — 1,550+ lounges: Centurion, Priority Pass, Plaza Premium, plus 10 Delta Sky Club visits/yr on Delta flights. From Jul 8, 2026, Centurion entry is within 5 hrs of departure and guests must be on your flight.

services.name: Global Dining Access & Platinum Nights by Resy
services.detail: Access to hard-to-get reservations at 1,300+ acclaimed restaurants worldwide, plus in-demand tables on select nights.

services.name: Platinum Card Concierge
services.detail: 24/7 help booking dining, tickets, and travel — call the number on the card.

services.name: Car Rental Privileges
services.detail: Complimentary elite status with Hertz (Gold Plus/President's Circle), Avis Preferred, and National Emerald Club Executive (enrollment required).

services.name: Cruise Privileges Program
services.detail: Book 5+ night cruises via Amex Travel for up to $300 shipboard credit/stateroom + amenities + 1 extra MR point/$.

services.name: International Airline Program
services.detail: Platinum Member Airfares — discounted premium-cabin international fares booked through Amex Travel.

services.name: By Invitation Only & No FX fees
services.detail: Access to exclusive events, plus zero foreign transaction fees on international spend.

additional_cards.title: Two ways to share it

benefits.text: Earns Membership Rewards on their spend (into your account)
Own Global Entry / TSA PreCheck credit
Can book Fine Hotels + Resorts / The Hotel Collection on the account
No lounge access on their own card
No hotel or car-rental elite status
Own Global Lounge Collection access (incl. 10 Sky Club visits)
Own Hilton + Marriott Gold & Leaders Club Sterling status
Car-rental elite status (Hertz Pres. Circle / Avis / National Exec.)
Own Global Entry credit + FHR/The Hotel Collection access
Does NOT get their own big statement credits

additional_cards.note: Up to 3 Additional Platinum cards ($195 each), users 13+. The $600 hotel credit and other statement credits stay with the primary account — so a $0 Companion card is plenty for a partner who just wants to spend + earn, while $195 makes sense only if they'll travel solo and use lounges.

timeline.text: Centurion Lounge access tightened: entry within 5 hours of departure only, and guests must be on the same flight.
$100 Saks credit permanently removed for everyone.
Etihad Guest dropped as a Membership Rewards transfer partner.
Uber VIP status retired, replaced by a lesser 'Signature Support.'
The $895 fee reaches existing cardholders at their first renewal on or after this date.
Emirates Skywards transfer ratio cut from 1:1 to 5:4.
Major refresh: fee $695 → $895; added Resy $400, Lululemon $300, Oura $200, Uber One $120, hotel credit to $600, and 2× on $5,000+ purchases.
Fee raised $550 → $695; the coupon book credit model begins.

cards.name: Sapphire Reserve

verdict.text: Best all-rounder if you use the credits

cards.earn_note: Strong bonus categories (dining and direct travel), but the 1× base still lags Venture X for uncategorized spend.

points.note: Legacy points earned before Oct 26, 2025 keep a 1.5¢ floor until Oct 26, 2027 — burn those first. Otherwise, transfer for the most value.

transfer_partners.highlight: World of Hyatt is the crown jewel — 1:1 and exclusive to Chase & Bilt, routinely 2–3¢+. Also Aeroplan, Virgin Atlantic, Flying Blue, Singapore. Wyndham added Feb 2026.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: Fewer airlines than Amex, but the hotel side (esp. Hyatt) is the strongest of any bank.

credits.name: Annual Travel Credit
credits.subtitle: auto-applies
credits.description: A flat statement credit against broad travel — airlines, hotels, tolls, parking, transit, rideshare.
tips: The single easiest credit in your whole wallet. It posts automatically against almost anything travel-coded.
Value it at the full $300 — it quietly drops the real fee to $495.

credits.name: Apple TV+ & Apple Music
credits.subtitle: through Jun 2027
credits.description: Complimentary Apple TV+ AND Apple Music — two separate services (~$250/yr combined). NOT the full Apple One bundle.
tips: Activate each service individually through Chase's app — they don't turn on together.
Already paying for either? Cancel the paid plan and switch to the complimentary one.
Android/Spotify household? Set the slider toward $0.

credits.name: The Edit Hotel Credit
credits.subtitle: $250 × 2 · 2-night min
credits.description: Semi-annual credit on ~1,400 luxury hotels via The Edit by Chase Travel. Breakfast for two + $100 property credit; upgrades/late checkout are availability-only (not guaranteed like FHR).
tips: Harder to spend than FHR: two-night minimum, smaller network, and prepaid-rate-only.
2026 adds a $250 promo at Select brands (IHG, Montage, Pendry, Omni, Virgin) — up to $500 back on one booking.
Set the slider to how many 2-night luxe stays you'll actually prepay.

credits.name: Dining Credit
credits.subtitle: $150 × 2 · Exclusive Tables
credits.description: Semi-annual credit, but ONLY at ~400 'Exclusive Tables' restaurants on OpenTable across ~26 cities.
tips: ~400 restaurants vs. Amex Resy's 10,000+. Hard to spend outside major metros or with dietary limits.
Vegas was gutted to one spot in mid-2026 — re-check first.

credits.name: StubHub / viagogo
credits.subtitle: $150 × 2 · enroll
credits.description: Semi-annual credit toward concerts and events.
tips: Enroll, then apply it to an event you'd attend anyway.
Compare against face value — resale markups can erase the 'credit.'

credits.name: DoorDash DashPass
credits.subtitle: + $10/mo non-restaurant
credits.description: Complimentary DashPass ($0 delivery fees) plus a $10/mo promo on non-restaurant (grocery/retail) orders.
tips: Activate DashPass by the deadline — it auto-covers delivery fees.
The $10/mo non-restaurant promo is use-it-or-lose-it — a monthly grocery DoorDash order captures it.

credits.name: Peloton
credits.subtitle: $10/mo + 10× gear
credits.description: Monthly credit toward a Peloton membership, plus 10× on equipment (up to 50k pts).
tips: Only meaningful if you're a subscriber.

cards.protection_note: The best travel insurance of the four — primary rental, real trip-cancellation limits, baggage delay, and emergency evacuation with a dollar limit. The one glaring gap: no cell-phone protection (Amex and Venture X have it).
cards.rental_note: The CSR includes free primary coverage (up to $75k) — it pays first, before your own auto policy. Decline the counter's CDW and pay with the card.

status_perks.name: IHG One Rewards Platinum
status_perks.note: Modest — some upgrades and bonus points; IHG elite is among the weakest hotel tiers.

status_perks.name: $75k spend unlocks
status_perks.note: IHG Diamond, World of Hyatt status, Southwest A-List + $500 credit, $250 Shops at Chase. Valuable, but only if you'd spend $75k anyway.

status_perks.name: Lounge access
status_perks.note: Chase Sapphire Lounges + Priority Pass — Priority Pass includes 2 guests free (unlike Amex).

services.name: Visa Infinite Concierge
services.detail: 24/7 concierge for reservations, tickets, and travel arrangements.

services.name: Visa Infinite Luxury Hotel Collection
services.detail: Elite-like perks (breakfast, upgrades, late checkout, property credit) at 900+ hotels — on top of The Edit.

services.name: DoorDash DashPass + Lyft
services.detail: Complimentary DashPass ($0 delivery fees) and 5× on Lyft rides through Mar 2027.

services.name: Points Boost
services.detail: Up to 2¢/point on select Chase Travel bookings — CSR's redemption edge.

services.name: Visa Infinite travel & roadside
services.detail: Travel & emergency assistance services and roadside dispatch, plus no foreign transaction fees.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Own Priority Pass Select + Chase Sapphire Lounge access
Complimentary DoorDash DashPass
Covered by Chase's travel & purchase protections
Earns Ultimate Rewards (into your account)
No separate statement credits

additional_cards.note: After the 2025 refresh, CSR authorized users cost $195 each — a real jump from the old $75. Worth it mainly if a travel companion needs their own lounge access; otherwise the primary can already bring 2 guests into Priority Pass for free.

timeline.text: Legacy 1.5¢ redemption on pre-Oct-2025 points expires — after this, only Points Boost or 1¢ remain.
Exclusive Tables dining list churned (net +27 restaurants but Las Vegas gutted to one).
Enhanced hotel credits begin.
New perks activate; the $795 fee (and $195 AU fee) apply at each renewal after this date.
Fee $550 → $795; new credits (Edit $500, dining $300, StubHub $300, Apple, DashPass). Flat 50% travel bonus replaced by Points Boost.
Debuts with a legendary 100k bonus and a $450 fee — the enthusiast benchmark.

cards.name: Venture X

earn_rates.category: Hotels & rental cars — via Capital One Travel
Flights & vacation rentals — via Capital One Travel

cards.earn_note: The flat 2× on everything is the quiet star — it beats the 1× base on both the Amex Platinum and Sapphire Reserve, with nothing to track.

points.note: Guaranteed 1¢ floor as a travel eraser on any travel charge — no other card here has that safety net.

transfer_partners.highlight: 15+ airline partners. Best sweet spots: Turkish (Star Alliance short-hauls), Flying Blue, Virgin Atlantic, JAL (added 2025), Wyndham.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: The gap vs. Chase: no Hyatt and no U.S. legacy airline — but the 1¢ eraser floor means miles are never worthless.

credits.name: Annual Travel Credit
credits.subtitle: Capital One Travel portal
credits.description: A $300 credit on bookings through the Capital One Travel portal, resetting each anniversary.
tips: Portal-only — book something you'd buy anyway. It price-matches within 24 hrs.
Resets on your anniversary, not Jan 1 — don't let it lapse.

credits.name: Anniversary Miles
credits.subtitle: 10,000 miles · auto-posted
credits.description: 10,000 miles (≥$100 toward travel) deposited automatically every anniversary — no spend required.
tips: Totally passive. With the $300 credit, that's ~$400 against a $395 fee — the card pays for itself.

credits.name: Premier Collection
credits.subtitle: luxury hotels · $100/stay
credits.description: A $100 experience credit plus upgrades and early check-in/late checkout on ~600 hotels via Capital One Travel (per stay, no annual cap).
tips: Triple-dip: the $100 experience credit stacks with your $300 annual travel credit and 10× earning on the same booking.
Per-stay, so value it by how many qualifying luxe stays you'll book.

cards.protection_note: Well-rounded for the price: primary rental, cell-phone, purchase AND return protection — it's the only one of the four with return protection. The catch is a low $2,000 trip-cancellation cap and no medical, so charge big prepaid or international trips to the Sapphire Reserve.
cards.rental_note: Like the CSR, the Venture X gives free primary coverage (up to $75k) — pays before your own policy. Between this and the CSR, you never need the Amex's paid Premium Car Rental Protection.

status_perks.name: Hertz President's Circle
status_perks.note: Top-tier Hertz status — better fleet and upgrades. Useful only if you rent often.

status_perks.name: Capital One Travel upgrades
status_perks.note: Room upgrades and check-in perks, but only on portal hotel bookings.

status_perks.name: Lounge access
status_perks.note: Capital One Lounges are strong and growing. From Feb 1, 2026 guests need $75k spend and authorized users cost $125 each for lounge access.

services.name: Visa Infinite Concierge
services.detail: 24/7 concierge for reservations, tickets, and arrangements.

services.name: Premier & Lifestyle Collections + Visa Infinite Hotels
services.detail: Luxury hotel perks ($100 experience credit, upgrades) via Capital One Travel and Visa Infinite.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees, plus Visa Infinite travel & roadside assistance.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Free to add — up to 4 authorized users
Hertz President's Circle status
Covered by trip & purchase protections
Earns 2× miles (into your account)
Lounge access now costs $125 each (from Feb 1, 2026)

additional_cards.note: Up to 4 authorized users are free — the best AU deal here. The only catch is the new $125-per-user lounge fee from Feb 2026; skip it for a user who won't use lounges and they still keep status + protections at no cost.

timeline.text: Lounge access tightened: authorized users cost $125 each, and free guest access now needs $75k annual spend.
Japan Airlines added as a transfer partner — a rare, high-value option for Japan travel.
Quietly stable; steadily added transfer partners while competitors churned benefits.
Debuts at $395 — dramatically undercutting the Amex Platinum and Sapphire Reserve.

cards.name: Delta SkyMiles Platinum

verdict.text: Keep only if you fly Delta with a companion

cards.earn_note: Fine for Delta spend, but you earn SkyMiles — the weakest currency here. For everyday spend, Venture X earns more and more flexibly.

points.note: Airline-locked and dynamically priced. Two escapes: top up from Amex MR (1:1), or book Delta metal via Virgin Atlantic for far fewer miles than SkyMiles charges.

transfer_partners.highlight: SkyMiles can't be transferred out — they're stuck in Delta's program.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: You can transfer INTO SkyMiles from Amex MR (1:1), Chase UR and Capital One — useful for topping up an award.

credits.name: Free Checked Bags
credits.subtitle: 1st + 2nd (domestic)
credits.description: First checked bag free on Delta flights (you + up to 8 on the reservation); second bag free domestic.
tips: ~$35 each way per bag per person — a family checking bags on one round trip clears most of the fee.
You don't even need to pay for the flight with this card.

credits.name: Rideshare Credit
credits.subtitle: $10/mo · enroll
credits.description: Monthly U.S. rideshare credit with Uber, Lyft, Curb, Revel, or Alto.
tips: Enroll, then one qualifying ride a month recovers the full $120.

credits.name: Resy Dining Credit
credits.subtitle: $10/mo · new in 2026
credits.description: Up to $10/month at 10,000+ U.S. Resy restaurants (via Global Dining Access by Resy) — added in the 2026 refresh.
tips: Stacks with your Amex Platinum's $400 Resy credit — two cards paying you at the same restaurants.
Monthly, no rollover; one Resy meal a month captures it.

credits.name: Companion Certificate
credits.subtitle: Main Cabin · annual
credits.description: Each year after renewal, one round-trip Main Cabin companion ticket to the U.S., Caribbean, or Central America — companion pays only taxes/fees ($22–$250).
tips: One qualifying round trip erases the $350 fee. Best on expensive routes — value the slider by the fare you'd save.
Book fare classes L, U, T, X or V — the cheapest basic buckets sometimes don't qualify.

credits.name: Delta Stays Credit
credits.subtitle: prepaid hotels · calendar year
credits.description: Up to $150/yr (calendar year) on prepaid hotels or vacation rentals booked through Delta Stays on delta.com.
tips: Resets on the calendar year, not renewal — a new cardholder can use it twice on one annual fee.
Use it on a Delta Stays booking you'd make anyway.

cards.protection_note: Thinner than the others — note the weaker $300/12-hour trip delay (vs. $500/6 hrs elsewhere) and secondary rental. It does keep Amex cell-phone protection and Premium Global Assist (incl. no-cost emergency evacuation coordination). Charge trips you want fully insured to the Sapphire Reserve.
cards.rental_note: Secondary only — it pays after your own auto policy. For rentals, your CSR or Venture X (free primary) is the better card to put it on.

status_perks.name: Medallion head start
status_perks.note: $2,500 MQD annually plus a spend boost — a meaningful shortcut to Silver/Gold if you're pursuing Delta status.

status_perks.name: Hertz Five Star
status_perks.note: Mid-tier Hertz status (counter skip, free additional driver, upgrades) — a step below Venture X's President's Circle.

status_perks.name: Lounge access
status_perks.note: None on its own (Sky Club needs the $650 Reserve). Your Amex Platinum already covers 10 Sky Club visits on Delta flights.

services.name: Free checked bags & priority boarding
services.detail: 1st bag free (worldwide) for you + up to 8 companions on the reservation; 2nd bag free domestic; Zone 5 priority boarding.

services.name: Complimentary upgrade queue
services.detail: Even without Medallion status, you're added to the upgrade list (below Medallion/Reserve members).

services.name: MQD Headstart, Boost & TakeOff 15
services.detail: $2,500 MQD head start, $1 MQD per $20 spent, and 15% off award flights booked with miles.

services.name: Global Dining Access by Resy & no FX fees
services.detail: The Resy credit runs through Global Dining Access; plus zero foreign transaction fees and Premium Global Assist.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Free to add — no annual fee for additional Platinum cards
1st free checked bag + priority boarding when flying Delta
Own Global Entry / TSA PreCheck credit
Earns SkyMiles (into your account)
Shares the account's credits — no separate set

additional_cards.note: Additional Delta Platinum cards are free, and each one extends the free-checked-bag + priority-boarding perk — so a travel companion on the account effectively gets their own free bag when you all fly Delta together.

timeline.text: No fee increase; added a free second checked bag (domestic), a $120 rideshare credit and a $120 Resy credit, raised Delta Stays to $150, and made the companion certificate work on Delta Vacations.
Delta Sky Club access restricted across Amex cards (visit caps introduced).
Fee raised $250 → $350; SkyMiles and Medallion shifted to a spend-based (MQD) model.
Standard co-brand with companion certificate and free first bag.
